commit.message:
Market event overhaul: 6 new events, bug fixes, QA hardening

- Add 3 new global events: yield curve inversion (recession multiplier), talent market shift (margin hit), private credit boom (rate reduction)
- Add 3 new portfolio events: cyber breach, antitrust scrutiny, competitor acquisition — each with 3 strategic choices
- Fix 10 sector event descriptions ("margin compression" → "revenue decline")
- Add wealth management positive event (Generational Wealth Transfer)
- Anti-repeat cooldown prevents back-to-back recession/crisis/credit tightening
- Portfolio events shuffle order each year for better variety
- PE Fund mode: quiet year flavor text, LP satisfaction deltas for all global events
- Filler tax strategy: permanent +1ppt margin boost (cost 200-350K, up from 150-250K)
- QA fixes: remove eventHistory double-push, add calculateMetrics to all 9 handlers, full sell cleanup for antitrust divest/fight (earnouts, rollover, bolt-ons, platforms, exitedBusinesses), fix antitrust double-charge, add event icons and negative classification to EventCard
- Update UserManualModal, changelog, display proofreader tests

commit.message:
Re-run sampling ablation with mean pooling, update Tables S2/S3

Re-ran all 11 strategies × 3 modalities × 10 folds with mean pooling
(previously used attention pooling). Key change: no strategy significantly
outperforms uniform baseline — mean pooling's uniform temporal weighting
already provides robust aggregation. Removed attention pooling footnotes
from Tables S2/S3 captions. Rewrote Section S1 narrative accordingly.

commit.message:
fix: persist approval nonce to work item for reconnect recovery (#612) (#647)

Closes #612

## Problem
Approval nonces were only broadcast via WS, never persisted. On
reconnect, pending_approvals was empty and work items stuck forever.

## Fix
- **turns.py**: `_build_approval_request_from_json()` builds full
approval dict from deferred tool requests
- **turns.py**: `_persist_turn_output()` now calls
`mark_waiting_for_approval()` with the approval_request dict (not just
mark_pending)
- **common.py**: TTL cleanup — stuck PENDING items with no
approval_request get cancelled after 1h (configurable via
`PENDING_APPROVAL_TTL_SECONDS`)
- **work_items.py**: Added `mark_waiting_for_approval()` method that
persists approval_request JSON

## Tests
- 15 new tests in `test_approval_nonce_persistence.py` covering: nonce
building, persistence, TTL cleanup, edge cases
- All 116+ existing tests still pass

Co-authored-by: Agent <agent@autopoiesis.ai>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename server.js to app.js to match Hostinger entry file config

commit.message:
Add SQLite memory system with FTS5 full-text search (#9)

* Add SQLite memory system with FTS5 full-text search

Implements a persistent memory system backed by SQLite with FTS5 for
full-text search. Memories can be saved/searched via agent tools and
auto-extracted from conversations via configurable middleware.

- MemoryStore: SQLite + FTS5 with configurable path, max size, and TTL
- DefaultMemoryExtractor: extracts [REMEMBER:] markers and user preferences
- memory_search / memory_save tools exposed to the agent
- Auto-extraction middleware (beforeLoopBegin prunes, afterLoopIteration extracts)
- Config: memory.enabled, path, maxSizeMB, ttlDays, extractor, autoExtract
- Env vars: RA_MEMORY_ENABLED, RA_MEMORY_PATH, RA_MEMORY_MAX_SIZE_MB, etc.
- Custom extractors via memory.extractor config path

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* Add layered memory (session + long-term), configurable patterns, and LLM reflection

Layered memory:
- Two memory layers: session (ephemeral, per-conversation) and long-term (durable)
- Session memories auto-expire via sessionTTLHours (default: 24h)
- Long-term memories persist with ttlDays (default: 90 days)
- promote() to upgrade session memories to long-term
- Size enforcement prioritizes removing session memories first

Configurable extraction patterns:
- PatternExtractor with configurable regex patterns, role filters, max length, capture modes
- DEFAULT_PATTERNS provides baseline (REMEMBER markers, user preferences)
- Custom patterns via config (merged with defaults)
- Deduplication within extraction passes

LLM-driven reflective extraction:
- ReflectiveExtractor sends conversation to an LLM for structured learning extraction
- Runs on afterLoopComplete (end of conversation, not per-iteration)
- Best-effort: never fails the main flow
- Configurable model (use a cheaper model for reflection)

New config options: sessionTTLHours, patterns[], reflect, reflectionModel
New tool: memory_delete
New env vars: RA_MEMORY_SESSION_TTL_HOURS, RA_MEMORY_REFLECT, RA_MEMORY_REFLECTION_MODEL

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* Use tool descriptions to drive proactive memory usage, no system prompt injection

The LLM discovers when to save/search memories purely from the tool
descriptions (which include PROACTIVELY directives). The middleware
stays focused on pruning and pattern extraction — no message injection.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* Make reflection prompt configurable

- ReflectiveExtractor accepts optional custom prompt (must contain {CONVERSATION})
- Export DEFAULT_REFLECTION_PROMPT for reference when writing custom prompts
- Config: memory.reflectionPrompt, env: RA_MEMORY_REFLECTION_PROMPT
- Thread through middleware options and src/index.ts wiring

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* Inject top-N recalled memories as user message at loop start

Instead of relying on the LLM to call memory_search, inject the top N
most recent long-term and session memories as a <recalled-memories> user
message at the start of each loop. This gives the LLM context without
extra tool calls.

- Configurable via memory.injectLimit (default: 20, set 0 to disable)
- Env var: RA_MEMORY_INJECT_LIMIT
- Long-term memories shown with tags, session memories tagged with session ID

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* Default memory destination is session; long-term is earned through reflection

Session is now the default layer for all memory operations:
- store.save() defaults to session
- memory_save tool defaults to session
- Pattern extractor saves to session
- Only the reflective extractor (afterLoopComplete) promotes to long-term

The reflection prompt is updated to set a high bar: only extract facts
with proven, lasting importance — reinforced preferences, architectural
decisions, recurring patterns.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* Remove memory_delete tool — LLM can't meaningfully produce memory IDs

Delete remains available on MemoryStore for programmatic use but is not
exposed as an agent tool.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* Simplify memory system to minimal core with memory_forget tool

Drop layers, extractors, and reflection in favor of a single flat store
with three tools (memory_save, memory_search, memory_forget). Fix forget()
returning inflated count from FTS triggers and list() ordering by
second-precision timestamp instead of ID.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* Trim memory system and add documentation

- Remove unused UPDATE trigger and buggy size-based enforceMaxSize loop
- Replace maxSizeMB with count-based maxMemories (reliable, no WAL issues)
- Combine schema init into single exec call
- Add memory section to README, configuration, tools, and concepts docs

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* Fix FTS5 query crashes and add robustness

- Guard search/forget against empty queries and FTS5 syntax errors
  (unmatched quotes, parens, bare operators) that would throw at runtime
- Add PRAGMA busy_timeout for safe multi-process access
- Add 12 new edge case tests: FTS special chars, empty queries,
  FTS sync after forget, persistence across reopen, trim/prune no-ops

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* Improve tool descriptions and injection for LLM effectiveness

- memory_save: directive language, concrete examples, dedup guidance
  (forget old version before saving updated one)
- memory_search: explains that recent memories are already injected,
  use this for targeted lookups beyond the recalled set
- memory_forget: broadened triggers to include contradictions and
  pre-update cleanup, not just explicit user requests
- Tags: suggest concrete categories (preference, project, convention,
  team, tooling) instead of vague "comma-separated tags"
- Query hints: "single keywords work best" to avoid FTS syntax issues
- Injection message: explains what recalled memories are and tells
  the LLM to forget outdated ones

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* Sync tools docs with updated memory tool descriptions

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* Add --memory flag and /memories, /forget REPL commands

- --memory CLI flag enables memory without editing config
- /memories [n] lists stored memories in the REPL
- /forget <query> lets users manually delete memories by search
- Update help text, README, and REPL docs

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* fix: increase /forget REPL command limit from 10 to 1000

The REPL /forget command called store.forget(query) without a limit,
defaulting to 10. Users expect /forget to delete all matching memories,
not just the first 10.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* feat: add --memories CLI flag to view stored memories

ra --memory --memories prints all stored memories and exits,
providing a quick way to inspect memory contents without starting
a REPL session.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* feat: add --forget with --dry-run and auto-enable memory for CLI flags

- `ra --memories` now auto-enables memory (no need for `--memory --memories`)
- `ra --forget "dark mode"` deletes matching memories and exits
- `ra --forget "dark mode" --dry-run` previews matches without deleting
- --forget and --memories both imply --memory automatically

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* refactor: simplify memory CLI — search with --memories, delete with --forget

Replace --dry-run workflow with a simpler pattern:
  ra --memories              # list all memories
  ra --memories dark mode    # search memories matching "dark mode"
  ra --forget dark mode      # delete memories matching "dark mode"

Users search first with --memories, then use the same query with --forget.
Both flags use positionals as the query and auto-enable memory.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* fix: make --memories and --forget accept quoted string queries

ra --memories "dark mode"   # search
ra --forget "dark mode"     # delete matches
ra --memories               # list all (no query)

Pre-extracts --memories/--forget from argv before parseArgs since
util.parseArgs doesn't support optional string values.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* chore: reduce default injectLimit from 20 to 5

Keeps context lean by default — inject only the 5 most recent
memories per loop. Users can increase via config if needed.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* fix: remove misleading "single keywords work best" from tool descriptions

FTS5 handles multiple keywords well (implicit AND). Updated search
and forget tool descriptions to show multi-keyword examples.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

* refactor: remove manual --memories/--forget parsing workaround

Replace hacky pre-extraction loop with standard util.parseArgs options.
--memories is now a regular string arg, --list-memories is a boolean
for listing all, and --forget is a regular string arg.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UES55H4Pujmi9K3ggjuosx

---------

commit.message:
fix(terminal): fix ERR trap $? capture and eliminate find|head pipe

The ERR trap had $(date) running before $? was captured, always logging
"exit 0" regardless of actual error code. Save $? to $_ec first.

Replace `find ... | head -1` with `find ... -print -quit` to eliminate
the pipe entirely — removes any pipefail + SIGPIPE edge case that may
have caused the intermittent Menu crash on VS Code reload.

commit.message:
Scale lens flare glow/spikes by stellar luminosity

Added luminosity field to star data objects (was only in class props).
StarFlare now computes a luminosity factor via log10 scale:
  M-class (L=0.04) → factor 0.45 — small dim glow, short spikes
  G-class (L=1.0)  → factor 0.70 — moderate baseline
  A-class (L=20)   → factor 0.96 — bright
  O-class (L=300K) → factor 1.80 — huge blazing flare

Factor scales glow radius, glow brightness, and spike length.
Gallery info now shows luminosity value for comparison.

Add decision module to all non-Go packages

Decision trees with mechanical and LLM evaluation, tree evolution from
intelligent to mechanical. DecisionOutcome, AuthorityLevel, IIntelligence
and IDecisionMaker interfaces, InternalNode/LeafNode tree structure,
evaluate() tree walker, detect_pattern/evolve for LLM-to-mechanical
evolution. Condition operators: equals, greater_than, less_than, exists,
matches, semantic. Parse outcome with fail-safe priority (deny > escalate
> permit > defer).

Python: 16 tests, TypeScript: 34 tests, .NET: 23 tests, Rust: 35 tests.
All existing tests continue to pass.

commit.message:
fix: allow advanceCard to reach cards.length so completion screen triggers (#13)

advanceCard was clamping to cards.length - 1, so isComplete (cardIndex >= cards.length) could never be true and the completion screen never showed.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

security: hardening pass — bash deny-list, config validation, path whitelist, PII scrubbing (#6)

* security(bash): add deny-list for destructive rm, mail, and DNS commands

Block rm -rf targeting /, ~, $HOME (with recursive flag variants).
Block mail/sendmail/mutt/postfix (data exfiltration).
Block dig/nslookup/host (DNS exfiltration).
Added to both executor deny-list and policy dangerous commands.

12 new executor tests, 8 new policy tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* security(policy): add validateConfig() with Zod schema + crash on missing policy

Executor now validates config at startup via SentinelConfigSchema.parse().
Empty classifications array triggers FATAL error — fail-fast, not silent.
Entrypoint: validateConfig() runs before Object.freeze().

5 new policy tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* security(executor): add allowedRoots path whitelist with symlink protection

New isPathAllowed() in path-guard.ts resolves symlinks via realpath before
checking against allowedRoots. Prevents symlink-based traversal attacks.
Applied to read_file, write_file, edit_file. createToolRegistry() accepts
allowedRoots param, passed from config in entrypoint and CLI.

8 new path-guard tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* security(types): add PII scrubbing patterns for SSN, phone, email, salary, URLs

New PII_PATTERNS array with 9 regex patterns derived from jobhunter envchain
fields. redactPII() and redactAll() (credentials + PII) added to types.
Executor credential-filter and audit redact now use redactAll().
Names, cities, titles deferred to NER evaluation in Phase 2.

14 new PII tests across executor and audit packages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update CLAUDE.md TODO + docker-compose network_mode rationale

Mark completed hardening items (bash deny-list, config validation,
path whitelist, PII scrubbing). Document network_mode: none deferral
rationale in docker-compose.yml. Update Phase 2 PII backlog with
deferred categories (ZIP, street, names — NER required).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* security: fix code review findings — fail-closed realpath, tightened PII/deny patterns

Critical fixes from 5-agent parallel code review:
- path-guard: realpath catch-all → ENOENT-only fallback; other errors deny access
- credential-patterns: salary regex requires comma-thousands ($150,000 yes, $5 no)
- credential-patterns: GitHub URL regex excludes repo URLs (profile-only via lookahead)

Important fixes:
- bash deny-list: structured {pattern, reason} with category-specific error messages
- bash deny-list: host/mail restricted to command position; rm restricted to root/home
- write-file: Docker branch uses guard.resolved (eliminates TOCTOU + redundant realpath)
- rules: validateConfig warns on unconfigured allowedRoots, errors on empty []
- chat.ts: now calls validateConfig (same gate as entrypoint)
- entrypoint: wraps validateConfig with human-readable error + process.exit(1)

Type improvements:
- path-guard: discriminated union return type (PathAllowed | PathDenied)
- credential-patterns: un-exported raw pattern arrays (only functions exported)
- Security logging: console.warn on path denials and bash blocks

Tests: 280 → 295 (15 new tests for false-positive coverage + ELOOP symlink)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: collaboration vs concurrent mode for AI results

- Add collaboration_id to ai_results for grouping cooperative tasks
- Collaboration groups: unified border, overlapping avatars, batch
  accept/reject ("整体采纳/回退"), no individual accept per result
- Concurrent results: independent accept/reject per result as before
- Add seed data: collab groups (classify+priority+language detect),
  historical results for history tab
- Add batch status API endpoint for group operations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

F6.1: Implement Session State Schema & Storage

Created comprehensive session management system with:

1. SessionMetadata dataclass:
   - Tracks session lifecycle (created_at, last_activity_at)
   - Timeout management (default 24 hours)
   - Message and nugget counts
   - User ID tracking

2. SessionMessage dataclass:
   - Conversation message with role (user/system)
   - Timestamps and optional metadata
   - Full conversation history

3. Session dataclass:
   - Complete session state
   - Current use case and expert tracking
   - Captured nuggets collection
   - Extra context storage
   - JSON serialization/deserialization

4. SessionManager class:
   - create_session(): Create new sessions with mode and timeout
   - save_session(): Persist to storage
   - load_session(): Retrieve with expiry checking
   - delete_session(): Clean up sessions
   - add_message(): Append to conversation
   - add_captured_nugget(): Store passive captures
   - list_sessions(): Filter by user/status
   - cleanup_expired_sessions(): Maintenance
   - get_session_summary(): Statistics

Features:
- File-based storage (JSON) with configurable path
- Multi-turn state persistence across interactions
- Session timeout handling (auto-cleanup)
- User-based session filtering
- Full audit trail via message history
- Graceful error handling

All functions tested and verified working correctly.
Ready for multi-turn conversation continuity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add shape stamp annotations with 10 shape types

New "Stamp" mode in the Annotate tab with line, arrow, rectangle, circle,
triangle, star, diamond, checkmark, x-mark, and heart shapes. Shapes support
configurable stroke color/width, optional fill color for closed shapes, and
a flip direction toggle for lines/arrows. Shapes use the same drag-to-move
and 8-handle resize system as text annotations, and render in downloaded PDFs.

feat: 实现 Host AI 主持人功能 + 时间线控制面板重设计

## Host AI 主持人功能
- 添加 hostModelId 状态管理，支持指定主持人模型
- 主持人模型在每轮讨论完成后进行汇总
- 支持 brainstorm/round/debate 三种模式的主持人汇总

## 控制面板时间线重设计 (v2.0)
- 将卡片布局改为紧凑的时间线日志格式
- 新增 generateTimelineEvents() 生成时间线事件
- 新增 renderTimelineEvent() 渲染各类事件
- 状态栏使用芯片单行显示所有模型状态
- 主持人汇总使用橙色边框特殊样式
- 添加深色模式适配

## 调试改进
- 添加详细日志追踪模型状态更新流程
- 添加 currentRound/totalRounds 日志输出
- 修复 setState() 后内容丢失的问题
